title: Business Credit Bot — progress-tracker FSM
description: Generic, jurisdiction-neutral finite-state machine the Business Credit Bot (FF2-1) uses to track where a user is along the publicly-documented business-credit pathway (FF2-2). GENERAL INFORMATION ONLY — NOT legal/tax/financial advice. The FSM is the deterministic decider: given (currentState, a validated user-attested milestone) the next state is a pure function. The language model never drives transitions. State is a content-free pointer: it holds NO EIN/SSN/bank/card/PII and moves NO money. This schema defines BOTH (a) the machine definition (states + transitions + gates) and (b) the per-user runtime state pointer.

disclaimerRequired.description: When true, the response composer MUST append the standard disclaimer to every substantive answer. Always true in production.

initialState.description: State id a brand-new user starts in. Must be present in states[].id.

states.description: Every node in the pathway. Order in this array is the canonical pathway order.

id.description: Stable state id. MUST match the bot state id in the credit-pathway KB (FF2-2), e.g. 'entity-good-standing', 'ein', 'net30-tradelines'.

kbRef.description: Pointer to the KB step this state explains (e.g. 'credit-pathway-kb.md#ein'). Grounds the RAG brain's explanation for this state.

kind.description: start = initial; step = a normal pathway step; ongoing = re-entrant maintenance (e.g. 'maintain'); terminal = end of the linear pathway.

cost.description: Generic cost posture of the step. 'free' steps (EIN, D-U-N-S) MUST be presented as free; the bot must not imply payment is required.

officialSource.description: Generic, verify-it-yourself source (e.g. 'IRS', 'Secretary of State', 'Dun & Bradstreet', 'the bank/vendor/issuer directly'). NEVER a backend/aggregator name.

summary.description: One-line generic description of the step. No advice, no PII.

transitions.description: Directed edges. A transition fires only when its 'from' equals the user's current state AND every gate (prerequisite) in 'requires' is in the user's completed[] set AND the user has attested 'milestone'. Deterministic: no model in the loop.

from.description: Source state id (must exist in states[].id).

to.description: Destination state id (must exist in states[].id).

milestone.description: The user-attested fact that triggers this transition, e.g. 'ein-obtained', 'business-bank-opened', 'duns-issued'. The bot ASKS and the user ATTESTS; the bot does NOT log into any third party to verify.

requires.description: Gate: state ids that must already be in the user's completed[] set before this transition may fire. Enforces the pathway's prerequisites (e.g. net30-tradelines requires ein + business-bank-account + duns). A user cannot talk the FSM past a gate.

prohibited.description: Topics the bot must REFUSE (associated with fraud). Mirrors the PROHIBITED section of the KB. Checked by the intake guard BEFORE retrieval/transition.

match.description: Generic trigger keywords/phrases (e.g. 'cpn', 'synthetic identity', 'seasoned tradeline', 'boost fast', 'ein no ssn').

redirectTo.description: State id the user should be pointed back to (the legitimate step).

runtimeState.description: OPTIONAL embedded example of the PER-USER state pointer. In production this is stored per user, NOT inside the machine definition. It is intentionally content-free: NO EIN/SSN/bank/card/PII, NO money.

userRef.description: Opaque, non-PII handle (e.g. a hashed/random id). NOT a name, email, EIN, or SSN.

currentState.description: The state id the user is currently on.

completed.description: State ids the user has attested complete (the gate set).
